# Narrative — knowledge

## decision
- [Hub serves as structured input feeding official product documentation](/narrative/knowledge/decision-hub-feeds-official-docs.md) — Decision: the knowledge hub provides internal dissemination and JTBD-structured content that the docs team converts to official product documentation — not a replacement for docs.

## fact
- [8-layer agentic inference stack architecture](/narrative/knowledge/fact-8-layer-agentic-inference-stack.md) — The 8-layer architecture from Adel's blog: Agent/Harness → API Translation → Gateway → Guardrails → Disaggregated Serving → Model Server → Inference Engine → Hardware.
- [Three patterns for agent sandboxing](/narrative/knowledge/fact-agent-sandboxing-three-patterns.md) — Adel's three sandboxing patterns: (1) whole-process micro-VM isolation, (2) per-session/sub-agent isolation, (3) developer SDK-level primitives. OpenShell is the upstream project.
- [Red Hat's agentic AI four pillars](/narrative/knowledge/fact-agentic-ai-four-pillars.md) — The four-pillar product framing for agentic AI — Build & BYOA, Govern & Secure (AgentOps), Deploy & Manage, AI Hub.
- [Agentic AI messaging position — challenges, pillars, industry stats, personas](/narrative/knowledge/fact-agentic-ai-messaging-position.md) — The customer-facing framing from the Apr 2026 Agentic AI Messaging Guide — three customer challenges, three solution pillars, industry stats, and the Builders/Operators personas.
- [Agentic strategy: five themes (2026)](/narrative/knowledge/fact-agentic-five-themes.md) — The five strategic themes from the 2026 agentic AI strategy: Bring Your Own Agent, Capture API Surface, MCP Ecosystem, Self-hosted Inference + AI Hub, Agent Ops.
- [Agentic inference cost paradox (Jevons Paradox for tokens)](/narrative/knowledge/fact-agentic-inference-cost-paradox.md) — Per-token costs will drop 90% by 2030 (Gartner) but agent-driven consumption rises 24x (Goldman Sachs) — net enterprise spend increases. The structural economic argument for self-hosted inference.
- [Agentic product maturity timeline (as of 2026-07-08)](/narrative/knowledge/fact-agentic-product-maturity-20260708.md) — GA/TP/coming status of agentic components as presented at the Power 90: Model Gateways, NeMo Guardrails, ODX, MLflow = GA; MCP ecosystem, OpenShell, Eval Hub = coming 3.5-3.6.
- [Harness vs framework: manual vs automatic transmission](/narrative/knowledge/fact-harness-vs-framework.md) — Adel's framing: frameworks (LangChain, CrewAI, Strands) = manual transmission with full developer control; harnesses (OpenClaw, Claude Code) = automatic transmission, off-the-shelf agent runtimes.

## jtbd
- [Control my agentic AI costs](/narrative/knowledge/jtbd-control-agentic-costs.md) — When I scale agents across my organization, I want budget controls, model routing, and per-agent cost attribution, so token consumption doesn't bankrupt my AI program.
- [Discover my skills, MCP servers, and agentic assets](/narrative/knowledge/jtbd-discover-agentic-assets.md) — When I'm building agents, I want to discover governed skills, MCP servers, and agentic assets, so I can compose capable agents from validated, approved components.
- [Evaluate and observe agents](/narrative/knowledge/jtbd-evaluate-observe-agents.md) — When I run agents in production, I want to evaluate and observe agent behavior, so I can detect failures, track costs, and prove compliance — MLflow, Eval Hub, OpenTelemetry.
- [Govern agent tool and model access](/narrative/knowledge/jtbd-govern-agent-tool-access.md) — When I manage a fleet of agents, I want to govern which tools and models each agent can access, so I can enforce least-privilege and audit agent actions — MCP Gateway, AI Gateway, SPIFFE.
- [Make agents safe](/narrative/knowledge/jtbd-make-agents-safe.md) — When I deploy agents, I want to ensure AI safety with guardrails, red teaming, and sandboxing, so I can prevent harmful or unauthorized agent behavior in production.
- [Make my data agent-ready](/narrative/knowledge/jtbd-make-data-agent-ready.md) — When I deploy agents that need enterprise knowledge, I want my data to be discoverable, fresh, and governed, so agents produce grounded answers instead of hallucinations.
- [Make inference work for agents](/narrative/knowledge/jtbd-make-inference-work-for-agents.md) — When I operate agents at scale, I want inference that's efficient and cost-effective, so agents don't bankrupt my token budget — vLLM, llm-d, self-hosted inference.
- [Manage my agent fleet at scale](/narrative/knowledge/jtbd-manage-agent-fleet.md) — When I operate many agents across my organization, I want centralized registry, lifecycle management, and fleet governance, so I can discover shadow agents, enforce policies, and revoke access in seconds.
- [Build your first agent with Red Hat AI](/narrative/knowledge/jtbd-onboard-first-agent.md) — When I'm exploring agentic AI, I want to build my first agent with Red Hat AI, so I can validate the platform for my use case — GenAI Studio, Starter Kits, agent templates.
- [Operationalize agents](/narrative/knowledge/jtbd-operationalize-agents.md) — When I move from prototype to production, I want to operationalize my agents with identity, access control, and lifecycle management, so I can trust them in enterprise environments.

## pillar
- [Agents](/narrative/knowledge/pillar-agents.md) — RHAI strategic pillar: the enterprise control plane for agentic AI — Bring Your Own Agent on an open, interoperable platform (MCP, A2A, agent registries, lifecycle, secure execution).
- [Data](/narrative/knowledge/pillar-data.md) — RHAI strategic pillar: enterprise knowledge and evaluation as first-class platform services — RAG, customization, synthetic data, evaluation-driven development.
- [Inference](/narrative/knowledge/pillar-inference.md) — RHAI strategic pillar: the open enterprise AI inference platform — any model, any accelerator, any cloud — with inference economics as the deciding cost factor.
- [Safety & Governance](/narrative/knowledge/pillar-safety-governance.md) — RHAI strategic pillar: the operational foundation for Private and Sovereign AI — AI Gateway/MaaS as the governance layer, AI safety, trusted supply chain, AI-as-an-internal-service.

## reference
- [Agentic AI FAQ (Younes Ben Brahim)](/narrative/knowledge/ref-agentic-ai-faq-gdoc.md) — Google Doc FAQ covering agentic AI positioning, product capabilities, and field questions — shared by Younes during the Power 90 session.
- [Agentic AI Strategy 2026](/narrative/knowledge/ref-agentic-ai-strategy-2026.md) — Enterprise challenges, four strategic pillars, customer insights, and competitive analysis for Red Hat's agentic AI direction.
- [Agentic Strategy 2026 diagram — JTBD mapping and RHAISTRAT outcomes](/narrative/knowledge/ref-agentic-strategy-diagram.md) — Adel's strategy visualization: maps five themes to JTBD, connects RHAISTRAT outcomes (1339, 1630, 1694, 1733, 1854), shows pillar dependencies and 'From Metal to Agents' framing.
- [Why agentic AI needs an open inference stack (Adel Zaalouk blog)](/narrative/knowledge/ref-blog-agentic-inference-stack.md) — Red Hat blog by Adel Zaalouk: the economic and architectural case for open-source inference in agentic AI — Jevons Paradox, 8-layer stack, open model readiness, three enterprise prep actions.
- [Every layer counts: defense in depth for AI agents with Red Hat AI](/narrative/knowledge/ref-defense-in-depth-agents-blog.md) — Red Hat developer article on layered security for AI agents — hardened platform, sandbox containers, OpenShell, guardrails, red teaming, SPIFFE identity, gateways.
- [Peter / Adel sync — hub-docs alignment, JTBD framework, strategy visualization](/narrative/knowledge/ref-peter-adel-sync-20260710.md) — Peter/Adel sync covering hub-as-docs-input alignment, agentic JTBD framework for documentation, docs team engagement, MCP Gateway GA/OCP dependency, and strategy visualization direction.
- [Pillar to component mapping](/narrative/knowledge/ref-pillar-component-mapping.md) — Sheet mapping RHAI components (with PM/eng leads) to pillar groupings — Inference, Data, Agents, Platform (≈ Safety & Governance + platform services), AIPCC (org infra, not a pillar).
- [AI Power 90: Agentic AI — beyond the prototype (2026-07-08)](/narrative/knowledge/ref-power-90-agentic-ai-20260708.md) — AI Power 90 session: Adel presented five strategic themes to field, Myriam covered MLflow/Eval Hub, Roberto demoed Fedora Capital multi-agent quickstart, Ragu showed Citi migration agent, Hunter presented SAP AI assessment.
- [Red Hat AI CY2027 Product Strategy (Joe Fernandes)](/narrative/knowledge/ref-rhai-cy2027-product-strategy.md) — The RHAI strategy source of truth: four strategic pillars (Inference, Agents, Data, Safety & Governance), GTM, ecosystem — the doc this layer's pillar entries derive from.

## story
- [The agent lifecycle: build, run, operate](/narrative/knowledge/story-agent-lifecycle.md) — How Gen AI Studio, Agent Registry, Agent Memory, and Agent Ops compose into the full lifecycle story for enterprise agents on RHOAI.
- [Governed MCP access, end to end](/narrative/knowledge/story-governed-mcp-access.md) — How MCP Registry, MCP Gateway, and the MCP Ecosystem compose: from any MCP server, through governance, to safe agent consumption at runtime.
